metrics.name: scholiq_courses_total
metrics.help: Number of Course objects in the Scholiq register.

metrics.name: scholiq_enrolments_total
metrics.help: Number of Enrolment objects in the Scholiq register.

metrics.name: scholiq_learner_profiles_total
metrics.help: Number of LearnerProfile objects in the Scholiq register.

pages.title: Dashboards
pages._note: Role-aware dashboard (ADR-009 §6): one component rendering exactly one CnDashboardPage, re-rendered per active role (admin/teacher/student) with an in-page switcher for multi-role users.

pages.title: Compliance
pages._note: Compliance overview surface (regulation coverage, attestations); officer/hr gated; no generic lib index/detail analogue.

pages.title: Course Detail
pages._note: A Course is a curricular container (parent of lessons, sessions, enrolments, assignments, assessments) — the education 'catalog/parent' archetype. Body leads with the course metadata (code, level, language, mandatory-training flag, regulation, curriculum-plan) as the 8-wide primary block with a compact Related panel resolving parent-course/curriculum-plan/programmes. Below sit the child rosters as related-index tables: Lessons (the syllabus), Enrolments (who is taking it), Cohorts (delivery groups) and Assignments — the four things a course owner actually manages. Course Materials render as a Files leaf (materials/handouts are the course's document surface). No email/calendar/talk: the Course schema declares NO linkedTypes and a course is a definition, not a correspondence thread (HARD RULE 1). Sidebar keeps metadata; lifecycle/change history lives on the audit-trail leaf there.

pages.title: Lesson Detail
pages._note: A Lesson is a single learning unit inside a course (ordered content item with a media contentRef) — the education content-item archetype. Body leads with lesson metadata (order, contentType, contentRef, duration, learning objectives, mandatory-training flag, regulation) 8-wide, with a Related panel resolving the parent Course alongside. Below, the lesson's Materials (files/media pointed at by lessonId) render as a related-index table plus a Files leaf, because a lesson IS its content and handouts. No comms leaves: Lesson declares no linkedTypes and is a definition, not a conversation (HARD RULE 1). Sidebar keeps metadata + an audit-trail leaf for content-change history.

pages.title: Lesson Player
pages._note: Interactive lesson playback (scene navigation, media controls, attempt tracking). No lib analogue.

pages.title: Enrolment
pages._note: An Enrolment links one learner to one course (learnerId, courseId, cohortId, mandatory flag, dueDate, source, manager, regulation) — the education enrolment/participation archetype. Body leads with enrolment metadata 8-wide with a Related panel resolving learner + course + cohort. The enrolment's academic footprint is surfaced as related-index tables filtered by learnerId + courseId: Grade entries, Submissions and Attendance records — the progress a coordinator checks on this enrolment. No email/calendar: the Enrolment schema declares no linkedTypes; learner correspondence belongs on the Learner profile, not on a join record (HARD RULE 1). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail (enrolment source/mandate changes are compliance-relevant).

pages.title: Bulk Enrol
pages._note: Mounts CnWizardDialog from @conduction/nextcloud-vue for the multi-step bulk-enrol flow (audience picker → course/section → confirm + batch progress).

pages.title: Regulation
pages._note: A Regulation is a compliance rule (applicability criteria, audience scope, annual-renewal + cycle, RAG thresholds) that mandatory courses map to via regulationSlug — the config/rule archetype. Body leads with the rule definition 8-wide + a Related panel. Its compliance footprint is shown as related-index tables of the records that cite this regulation's slug: Attestations (proof-of-completion) and External training records, plus the mandatory Courses bound to it — what a compliance officer audits. No comms/files: a regulation is a policy definition, not a document or conversation (HARD RULES 1-2); attestations carry the evidence. Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail (threshold/renewal changes are auditable policy edits).

pages.title: Attestation
pages._note: An Attestation is an immutable proof-of-completion record (learner, course/lesson, regulation, score, signature, signing key, actor IP, xAPI statement) — the education document/record archetype, read-only. Body leads with the attestation fields 8-wide + a Related panel resolving learner + course + lesson + regulation. Signed evidence renders as a Files leaf below (the attestation IS a signed document a compliance officer downloads). No email/calendar: an attestation is a sealed record, not a conversation (HARD RULE 1). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail — the tamper-evidence trail is the whole point of an attestation.

pages.title: Export audit pack
pages._note: Mounts CnExportWizard from @conduction/nextcloud-vue for the compliance audit-pack export (regulation + date-range + server-side bundle).

pages.title: Credential
pages._note: A Credential is an issued certificate/badge (learner, course, kind, issued/expires, issuer DID, signature, OpenBadges3 + EDCI payloads, verification URL, revocation reason) — the education document/attestation archetype. Body leads with issuance metadata 8-wide + a Related panel resolving learner + course + issuing enrolment. The signed badge artefacts render as a Files leaf (a credential IS a downloadable/verifiable document). No email/calendar: a credential is a sealed award, not correspondence (HARD RULE 1). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail — issuance and revocation events are the credential's legal record.

pages.title: Verify Credential
pages._note: Public verification view of a Credential (mode:public) — a third party checking authenticity, NOT an internal editor. Deliberately minimal: a single full-width Data block showing only the verifiable claims (kind, learner, course, issued/expires, issuer DID, revocation status) plus a Files leaf for the signed badge artefact. No related-index, no comms, no audit sidebar — a public verifier must see the assertion and its signature and nothing about internal relations or history (HARD RULE 1, minimal-surface). Sidebar disabled: this is an anonymous proof page.

pages.title: My learning
pages._note: Learner-facing home surface (enrolled courses, progress, next activities); no generic lib index/detail analogue.

config._note: A Programme is a qualification-level container (level, code, curriculum-plan, member courses, credential template) — the education 'parent/catalog' archetype. Body leads with programme metadata (8-wide) and a Related panel resolving the curriculum-plan and credential template. Below, the constituent Courses and delivery Cohorts render as related-index rosters — the two things a programme owner manages — and Final grades give an outcome roster. No comms leaves: Programme declares no linkedTypes and is a definition, not a correspondence thread (HARD RULE 1). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail change history.

A Curriculum Plan defines the structure and pass-rules of a qualification (required/elective courses, components, formula, grade-scale, pass rules, periods) — a config/structure archetype. Body leads with the plan definition (8-wide) plus a Related panel resolving the grade-scale and owning programmes. Below, the Grade entries and Final grades computed against this plan render as related-index tables so an examinations officer can audit outcomes. No comms leaves (no linkedTypes; this is a rule definition). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail for rule-change history.

A Cohort is a delivery group (a course/programme run for a set of learners in an academic year/period, mapped to an NC group). Body leads with cohort metadata (8-wide) and a Related panel resolving programme, course and NC group. The three things a cohort coordinator manages render as related-index rosters: scheduled Sessions, Enrolments (the roster) and Attendance flags (early-warning). No comms leaves (no linkedTypes). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail.

pages.title: Cohort timetable
pages._note: Mounts CnTimelineView from @conduction/nextcloud-vue for the date-grouped session timetable (calendar-like layout).

config._note: A Session is a scheduled teaching occurrence (start/end, location, tied to cohort/course/lesson, with materials and assignments). Body leads with session details (8-wide) and a Related panel resolving cohort/course/lesson. The attendance roster is the primary operational surface, so Attendance records render as a wide related-index; assigned work shows as an Assignments table. Session materials render as a Files leaf (handouts for the session). No calendar/email leaf: Session declares no linkedTypes despite being time-boxed (HARD RULE 1). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail.

A Material is a learning resource (kind, file reference or URL, license, LOM tags) attached to a course/lesson/session — a document/asset archetype, so it is files-first. Body leads with the actual file/preview via a wide Files leaf, then the material metadata (kind, license, LOM tags, order) below, with a Related panel resolving the owning course/lesson/session. No comms leaves (no linkedTypes). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail for version history.

A Rubric is a scoring scaffold (named criteria + max points) reused across assignments — a config/definition archetype. Body leads with the rubric definition including the criteria list (8-wide) and a Related panel. Below, the Assignments that reference this rubric render as a related-index so a designer sees reuse. No comms leaves (no linkedTypes). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail.

An Assignment is gradable work (instructions, due date, max points, rubric, late/plagiarism policy) belonging to a course/session/cohort. Body leads with assignment details (8-wide) and a Related panel resolving course/session/rubric. The Submissions roster is the operational heart, rendered as a wide related-index, with a Submissions-received KPI stat. Briefing materials render as a Files leaf. No comms leaves (no linkedTypes). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail.

A Submission is a learner's handed-in work for an assignment (attachments, feedback, rubric scores, proposed grade, linked grade entry) — a result/score record. Body leads with the submission data including feedback and rubric scores (8-wide), a proposed-grade KPI stat, and a Related panel resolving the assignment and grade entry. The submitted work renders as a Files leaf (attachments are the point of a submission). No comms leaves (no linkedTypes). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail.

pages.title: Submit work
pages._note: Mounts CnRichSubmitDialog from @conduction/nextcloud-vue for assignment submission (file upload + late-submission rules).

pages.title: Mark submission
pages._note: Grading UI (rubric scores, inline feedback, attachments). Lib gap: grading workflow widget.

config._note: An Item Bank is a reusable pool of assessment questions grouped by subject. Body leads with the bank metadata (8-wide) and a Related panel. The constituent Items render as a wide related-index (the bank's actual content), with a total-items KPI stat. No comms leaves (no linkedTypes). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail.

An Item is a single assessment question (interaction type, QTI body, correct response, max score, difficulty, subject tags). Body leads with the item definition including the QTI body (8-wide) and a Related panel resolving the owning item-bank. No child rosters (an item is a leaf). No comms leaves (no linkedTypes). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail for revision history.

An Assessment is a configured test (item refs, scoring scheme, pass mark, time limit, attempts, proctoring, availability window) tied to a course/session/cohort. Body leads with the assessment configuration (8-wide) and a Related panel resolving course/session and grade-entry component. Learner attempts render as a wide Results related-index, with an attempts KPI stat. No comms leaves (no linkedTypes). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail.

An Assessment Result is one learner's attempt (responses, timing, proctoring session, grade entry). Body leads with the result data including responses (8-wide) and a Related panel resolving the assessment, learner and grade entry. The proctoring evidence for this attempt renders as a related-index. No comms leaves (no linkedTypes). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail.

A Proctoring Session records exam-monitoring for one assessment attempt (provider, status, recorded artefacts, integrity flags) — an evidence archetype. Body leads with the session data including flags (8-wide) and a Related panel resolving the assessment result and learner. Recorded artefacts (video/screen captures) render as a wide Files leaf — the evidence surface. No comms leaves (no linkedTypes). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail as a tamper log.

pages.title: Take assessment
pages._note: Assessment-taking UI (timer, item navigation, autosave, proctoring hooks). No lib analogue.

pages.title: Item author
pages._note: Assessment item authoring (item-bank editor, response key, scoring rules). No lib analogue.

pages.title: Proctoring review queue
pages._note: Proctoring review queue with playback annotations + flagged events. No lib analogue.

pages.title: Import QTI package
pages._note: Mounts CnWizardDialog from @conduction/nextcloud-vue for the QTI item-bank import (file upload → parse → preview → commit).

config._note: A Grade Scale defines how scores map to grades (kind, bands, min/max, pass threshold, rounding) — a config archetype. Body leads with the scale definition including bands (8-wide) and a Related panel. Grade entries recorded against this scale render as a related-index so an examinations officer sees usage. No comms leaves (no linkedTypes). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail for rule-change history.

A Grade Entry is a single recorded score (value, scale, weight, period, grader, source submission/result) for a learner and curriculum component — a score record. Body leads with the entry data including value and comment (8-wide), a value KPI stat, and a Related panel resolving the learner, course, grade-scale and source submission/result. No comms leaves (no linkedTypes). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail as the grade-change audit.

A Final Grade is the computed outcome for a learner on a course/programme (value, passed flag, breakdown, last recompute time) — an aggregate result. Body leads with the final-grade data including the breakdown (8-wide), a value KPI stat and a passed KPI, plus a Related panel resolving learner/course/programme/plan. The contributing Grade entries render as a related-index for traceability. No comms leaves (no linkedTypes). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail.

pages.title: Gradebook
pages._note: Mounts CnDataMatrix from @conduction/nextcloud-vue for the gradebook (cohort × plan inline-edit grid with weighted aggregation).

pages.title: Grade impact
pages._note: Grade impact detail (factor breakdown + override history). Could be type:"detail" once secondary panels lift into widgets.

config._note: A Learning Plan Template defines the structure of individual learning/support plans (sections, goal domains, required signer roles, review cadence) — a config/template archetype. Body leads with the template definition (8-wide) and a Related panel. Learning plans instantiated from this template render as a related-index so a coordinator sees adoption. No comms leaves (no linkedTypes). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail.

A Learning Plan is an individual learner's development/support plan (goals, support measures, review cadence, coordinator, versioning). Body leads with the plan data including goals and support measures (8-wide) and a Related panel resolving learner, template, cohort/course and coordinator. Its Evaluations (periodic reviews) render as a wide related-index — the plan's living history — and supporting documents render as a Files leaf. No comms leaves (no linkedTypes). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail.

A Learning Plan Evaluation is a periodic review of a learning plan (goal outcomes, narrative, attendees, next review date). Body leads with the evaluation data including goal outcomes and narrative (8-wide) and a Related panel resolving the parent plan and attendees. Supporting documents (minutes/evidence) render as a Files leaf. No comms leaves (no linkedTypes). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail.

A Signature is a cryptographic/attested sign-off on a subject object (subject kind/id/version, signer, role, assurance level, method, evidence ref) — an attestation/evidence archetype, files-first. Body leads with the signed-evidence artefact via a Files leaf, then the signature metadata (signer, role, assurance, method) and a Related panel resolving the signed subject. No comms leaves (no linkedTypes). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail as a tamper log.

pages.title: Sign learning plan
pages._note: Mounts CnSignatureCapture from @conduction/nextcloud-vue for the learning-plan signature flow (canvas/typed signature + audit).

pages.title: Learning plan editor
pages._note: Mounts CnRelationshipGraph from @conduction/nextcloud-vue for the learning-plan editor (dependency-chain layout, drag-drop ordering, milestone scoring).

config._note: An Attendance Record is a single presence mark (status, minutes attended, marked by/at, reason, linked excuse) for a learner in a session. Body leads with the record data (8-wide) and a Related panel resolving session, learner, cohort and any linked excuse request. Supporting evidence renders as a Files leaf. No comms leaves (no linkedTypes). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail as the mark-change audit.

An Excuse Request is an absence justification (date range, reason kind, attachment, submitter auth level, decision) — a request/case archetype. Body leads with the request data including reason and decision (8-wide), a Related panel resolving learner and submitter, and the supporting attachment as a wide Files leaf (the evidence). Attendance records covered by this excuse render as a related-index. No comms leaves (no linkedTypes). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail.

An Attendance Threshold is a rule (kind, scope, window, metric, limit, on-cross action) that triggers early-warning flags — a config/rule archetype. Body leads with the rule definition (8-wide) and a Related panel resolving the scoped cohort. The Attendance flags this rule has raised render as a wide related-index so a mentor sees its effect. No comms leaves (no linkedTypes). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail for rule-change history.

An Attendance Flag is a raised early-warning (learner, threshold, window, metric value, breaching records, mentor) — an alert/case archetype. Body leads with the flag data including the metric value (8-wide), a metric KPI stat, and a Related panel resolving learner, threshold, cohort, mentor and any data-exchange job. The breaching Attendance records render as a related-index (the evidence). No comms leaves (no linkedTypes). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail.

pages.title: Mark attendance
pages._note: Mounts CnDataMatrix from @conduction/nextcloud-vue for roster-based attendance marking (inline-edit grid with bulk + per-student state + late codes).

pages.title: Submit excuse
pages._note: Mounts CnRichSubmitDialog from @conduction/nextcloud-vue for excuse submission (file attachment + reason taxonomy).

config._note: A Data Mapping Profile is an integration config (target, direction, source/target schema, field mappings, validation) for data exchange — a technical/config archetype. Body leads with the profile definition including field mappings (8-wide) and a Related panel. The Data exchange jobs run against this profile render as a wide related-index (the run log). NO comms leaves — this is machine config, not correspondence (HARD RULE 1). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail.

A Data Exchange Job is a single import/export run (direction, target, mapping profile, scope, format, timing, result, connector run, error) — a technical/run-log archetype. Body leads with the job data including result and error message (8-wide) and a Related panel resolving the mapping profile, requester and originating flag. The result payload/log renders as a Files leaf. NO comms leaves (machine job, not correspondence). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail.

pages.title: Request export
pages._note: Mounts CnExportWizard from @conduction/nextcloud-vue for the data-exchange export request (regulation + scope + delivery channel).

pages.title: OSO dossier review
pages._note: Mounts CnStructuredDocReview from @conduction/nextcloud-vue for the OSO dossier review (XML preview + validation + sign-off).

config._note: A Learner Profile is the person record (names, birth date, encrypted BSN, school, ECK id, affiliation, roles, parents, manager, department) — the person archetype. Body leads with the profile data (8-wide) and a Related panel resolving parents and manager. The learner's Enrolments, Learning plans and Final grades render as related-index rosters — the operational surfaces a mentor needs. No comms leaves on the body: LearnerProfile declares no linkedTypes (HARD RULE 1). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail.

An xAPI Statement is an immutable learning-experience event (actor, verb, object, result, context, timestamp, authority) — a log/technical archetype. Body leads with the statement data including the full actor/verb/object/result JSON (wide, 12-col, single column for readability) and a Related panel resolving the linked course/lesson. NO comms leaves, NO child rosters — an xAPI statement is a write-once event. Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail.

pages.title: School-year rollover
pages._note: Mapping editor for the jaarovergang (school-year-rollover): the "custom view only where a manifest page cannot render it" exception. Proposes a leerjaar-increment default mapping, runs the mandatory side-effect-free preview gate, and triggers the idempotent server-side execution (RolloverExecutionHandler) by transitioning the RolloverPlan to executing.

config._note: An External Training Record is a learner's externally-completed training awaiting verification (title, provider, kind, regulation, completion/validity dates, evidence, verifier, resulting credential) — a request/evidence archetype. Body leads with the record data including verification status (8-wide), a Related panel resolving learner, linked course and resulting credential, and the certificate/evidence as a wide Files leaf (the point of the record). No comms leaves (no linkedTypes). Sidebar keeps metadata + audit-trail as the verification audit.
